prevalence.source: ISG (Indian Society of Gastroenterology) & ICMR surveys
prevalence.trend: increasing due to urbanization and stress

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV criteria adapted for India, ISG guidelines emphasizing clinical history and exclusion of organic disease

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 45% moderate due to limited specialist access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ISG/Rome IV criteria, clinical diagnosis, limited colonoscopy access
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary: spice reduction, fiber; Medical: antispasmodics (mebeverine), rifaximin for IBS-D; Ayurvedic: Triphala, Hingvastak churna for digestion
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Post-infectious IBS common, spicy diet culture, limited access to low FODMAP education, overlap with functional dyspepsia
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: ISG guidelines, affordable generic medications, Ayurvedic digestive formulas, growing urban awareness
